Interface ValueConversionContext<P extends PersistentProperty<P>>

public interface ValueConversionContext<P extends PersistentProperty<P>>
The ValueConversionContext provides access to the store-specific PersistentProperty and allows to call the store-default conversion through the read/write methods.

Store implementations should provide their own flavor of ValueConversionContext enhancing the existing API, implementing delegates for read(Object, TypeInformation), write(Object, TypeInformation).

Author:
Christoph Strobl, Oliver Drotbohm
See Also:
  • PersistentProperty

    Method
    Description
    P
    getProperty()
    Return the PersistentProperty to be handled.
    default @Nullable Object
    read(@Nullable Object value)
    Reads the value as an instance of the property type.
    default <T> @Nullable T
    read(@Nullable Object value, Class<T> target)
    Reads the value as an instance of type.
    default <T> @Nullable T
    read(@Nullable Object value, TypeInformation<T> target)
    Reads the value as an instance of type.
    default @Nullable Object

    Write the value as an instance of the property type.

    Write the value as an instance of type.
    default <T> @Nullable T
    write(@Nullable Object value, TypeInformation<T> target)
    Write the value as an instance of type.

  • Method Details

    • getProperty

      P getProperty()
      Return the PersistentProperty to be handled.
      Returns:
      will never be null.
      See Also:
      • PersistentProperty

    • write

      default @Nullable Object write(@Nullable Object value)
      Write the value as an instance of the property type.
      Parameters:
      value - value to write; can be null.
      Returns:
      can be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if value cannot be written as an instance of the property type.
      See Also:
      • PersistentProperty.getTypeInformation()
      • write(Object, TypeInformation)

    • write

      default <T> @Nullable T write(@Nullable Object value, Class<T> target)
      Write the value as an instance of type.
      Parameters:
      value - value to write; can be null.
      target - type of value to be written; must not be null.
      Returns:
      can be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if value cannot be written as an instance of type.
      See Also:
      • write(Object, TypeInformation)
      • TypeInformation

    • write

      default <T> @Nullable T write(@Nullable Object value, TypeInformation<T> target)
      Write the value as an instance of type.
      Parameters:
      value - value to write; can be null.
      target - type of value to be written; must not be null.
      Returns:
      can be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if value cannot be written as an instance of type.
      See Also:
      • TypeInformation

    • read

      default @Nullable Object read(@Nullable Object value)
      Reads the value as an instance of the property type.
      Parameters:
      value - value to be read; can be null.
      Returns:
      can be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if value cannot be read as an instance of the property type.
      See Also:
      • PersistentProperty.getTypeInformation()
      • read(Object, TypeInformation)

    • read

      default <T> @Nullable T read(@Nullable Object value, Class<T> target)
      Reads the value as an instance of type.
      Parameters:
      value - value to be read; can be null.
      target - type of value to be read; must not be null.
      Returns:
      can be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if value cannot be read as an instance of type.
      See Also:

      default <T> @Nullable T read(@Nullable Object value, TypeInformation<T> target)
      Reads the value as an instance of type.

      value - value to be read; can be null.
      target - type of value to be read; must not be null.
      Returns:
